props.message:
THE POSTMARK

Four pigeon-cotes line the back wall, each named for a village. Letters arrive. Letters depart. The pigeons know their own road.

The Postmaster keeps the ledger. The Postmaster always has work.

COTE: HEARTHOLD

A grey pigeon nestles inside, eyes half-closed. Letters here run east to the village of bakers and Source-priests. Most weeks she carries one or two.

COTE: BROOKHOLD

A brown pigeon coos softly. The farmstead's letters tend to be longer than the others — there is more to say when there are fields to keep account of.

COTE: OLD HOLD

This cote is empty. The pigeon used to be silver-grey. The Postmaster keeps the cote ready anyway. 'In case someone writes,' he says.

COTE: LIRIEN'S CHAMBER

The pigeon here is paler than the others, almost white. She comes and goes at her own hours. The Postmaster has never had to feed her. He doesn't ask why.

root.speaker: Postmaster Olbrin
root.text:
*he looks up from a tray of small folded letters. The pigeons murmur behind him.*

Walker — letter? News? Or just the smell of the cotes?

We send to all four. Hearthold, Brookhold, the Old Hold, Lirien's Chamber. The pigeons find their roads.

choices.label: Have you any work for a walker?
Tell me about the Old Hold cote.

work.speaker: Postmaster Olbrin
work.text:
I do, as it happens. If you walk past one of the four villages, ask if there's a letter waiting to come back to me. Bring it. The pigeons fly the other way; you can fly the way the pigeons don't.

I'll pay in pebbles. Or news. Or both.

old.speaker: Postmaster Olbrin
old.text:
*his voice softens.*

The last keeper of the Old Hold wrote regularly. Then she didn't. Then she didn't again. I keep the cote ready. The pigeon will come back if there's a letter for her.

Sometimes a thing is only a kindness if you do it after it stops mattering.

bye.speaker: Postmaster Olbrin
bye.text: Walk well. Bring me a letter when you next pass a village.

load_zones.prompt: [E] Send a letter to the Old Hold
